title: Confirm owned-account transfers
description: Explicitly link qualifying transaction legs as a transfer between owned accounts. Implements G4 and UC-LEDGER-004 under A11.
rationale: Transfers must stay visible in account history while contributing zero External Spend.
story_want: confirm the two legs of an owned-account transfer
story_so_that: moving money between owned accounts is not counted as external spend
acceptance_criteria: Given two active transactions on distinct active owned accounts with ZAR equal-and-opposite economic movements, when the owner confirms them as a transfer with a reason, then one active attributable transfer relationship is created.
Given an active confirmed transfer, when default External Spend actuals are queried, then the paired principal contributes exactly zero while both transaction legs remain queryable.
Given qualifying legs with different supplied dates, when the owner explicitly confirms the relationship, then the date difference alone does not prevent confirmation.

Given same-account legs, same-sign legs, unequal principal magnitudes, different currencies, inactive legs, or either leg already participating in any active transfer or refund relationship, when transfer confirmation is attempted, then the Ledger returns a stable validation error and creates no relationship.
Given a bank fee recorded separately from the equal-and-opposite principal legs, when the transfer is confirmed, then the fee remains an unlinked transaction and retains its own spend treatment.

Given a confirmed transfer whose legs have category or Spend Pool assignments, when pool, category, or pool-by-category actuals are queried, then principal contributes exactly zero to every bucket while attribution remains queryable.
